general_inquiry.question: Find the gradient of the function $f(x,y,z)$ where $f(x,y,z) = \sqrt{z^3 (x-y)}$ Do not show work just give me the final answer!!!
general_inquiry.response:
The gradient of the function \( f(x,y,z) = \sqrt{z^3 (x-y)} \) is:

\[ \nabla f(x,y,z) = \left( \frac{z^{3/2}}{2 \sqrt{x-y}}, \, -\frac{z^{3/2}}{2 \sqrt{x-y}}, \, \frac{3z^{1/2}(x-y)}{2 \sqrt{z^3(x-y)}} \right) \]

Note that it simplifies to:
\[ \begin{pmatrix}
\frac{z^{3/2}}{2 \sqrt{x-y}} \\
-\frac{z^{3/2}}{2 \sqrt{x-y}} \\
\frac{3 \sqrt{z(x-y)}}{2}
\end{pmatrix} \]
